segments.p1-5-g21: Это — demonstrative pronoun pointing to the situation just mentioned. Уже is an adverb meaning “already.” Было is the past tense of быть used as a linking verb to state a past condition. Всего here is an adverbial intensifier placed before the comparative, and гаже is an older comparative form meaning “worse” (colloquial/poetic register). Together the phrase says that the situation had grown considerably worse.
segments.p1-5-g22: При этом is a connective phrase meaning “in doing this / at the same time” that links this behavior to the prior context. Я is the subject. Опять-таки is a colloquial two-part adverb meaning “once again / yet again” (опять + таки = repeated action/emphatic). Душою is the old instrumental form of душа (‘with the soul’), used adverbially to mean emotionally/wholeheartedly, and умилялся is the past tense, reflexive/imperfective verb ‘to be moved, to feel tenderness’ describing the speaker’s inner feeling.
segments.p1-5-g23: Раскаивался is the first-person past imperfective reflexive form of раскаиваться, ‘to repent / express remorse.’ The imperfective past implies an ongoing or habitual inward action and it is given as one item in a series of actions.
segments.p1-5-g24: Слезы проливал is a common collocation meaning ‘shed tears’; слезы is the direct object in the accusative and проливал is the past imperfective verb ‘was shedding’ (descriptive, part of the series). The coordinating и links this verb phrase to the following ones.
segments.p1-5-g25: Конечно is a parenthetical adverb meaning ‘of course,’ inserted to qualify what follows; it modifies the speaker’s attitude toward the ensuing action.
segments.p1-5-g26: Самого себя is an emphatic reflexive accusative (самого intensifies себя) meaning ‘oneself/the very self.’ Надувал is past imperfective of надувать; here it carries the idiomatic sense of ‘puffing oneself up / deceiving or flattering oneself,’ used as another item in the series of inner behaviours.
segments.p1-5-g27: Хоть и is a concessive conjunction meaning ‘although / even though.’ Вовсе не is a strong negation meaning ‘not at all,’ and притворялся is the past imperfective reflexive ‘to pretend / feign.’ Together the clause concedes that, despite the inward displays, he did not outwardly pretend at all.
segments.p1-5-g28: Сердце is the nominative subject (here referring to feelings, not the organ). Уж is an emphatic adverb meaning ‘already’ or ‘by now.’ Тут means ‘here’ in the sense ‘at this point / at the moment’ (not literal place). Как-то is a particle combination (‘somehow / sort of’) that softens the assertion, and гадило is the past neuter of гадить used figuratively in colloquial/pejorative register to mean ‘behaved nastily / made one feel bad.’
segments.p1-5-g31: Тут уж даже и is a string of adverbs/particles meaning ‘at this point / already / even,’ used to emphasize impossibility. Законов природы is a genitive plural noun phrase ‘the laws of nature’ (genitive used after a negation/impersonal construction). Нельзя is an impersonal modal ‘it is not possible / one cannot,’ было is the past tense auxiliary that often appears with нельзя, and обвинить is the infinitive ‘to accuse / to blame.’ The construction нельзя было обвинить literally means ‘it was impossible to accuse/blame.’
segments.p1-5-g32: Хотя is a concessive conjunction ‘although.’ Все-таки is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning ‘all the same / nevertheless.’ Законы природы is here the nominative plural subject ‘the laws of nature.’ Постоянно is ‘constantly,’ и is a simple coordinating particle ‘and’ used here as a mild linker, более всего is a comparative phrase meaning ‘more than anything,’ всю жизнь is a time adverbial ‘all (my) life’ (всю is accusative feminine modifying жизнь), меня is the accusative object ‘me,’ and обижали is the past imperfective plural ‘offended / wronged / mistreated,’ agreeing with the plural subject. The whole clause says that, despite the impossibility of blaming nature for this moment, the laws of nature had nevertheless constantly wronged him throughout his life.
segments.p1-5-g125: Это is a neuter demonstrative pronoun used as the sentence's subject/pointing word ('this/these'). Уже опять is an adverbial cluster placed before the predicate and acting as a parenthetical modifier meaning 'already/once again' — the two adverbs reinforce each other for emphasis.
segments.p1-5-g126: Стало быть is a fixed impersonal phrase meaning 'therefore/so/it follows that'; grammatically it behaves like a parenthetical connector and does not introduce an explicit finite verb form here, but links the initial demonstrative and adverbials to the nominal predicate that follows.
segments.p1-5-g127: Законы природы is the nominal predicate: законы is nominative plural ('laws'), and природы is genitive singular modifying it ('of nature'). Because Russian often omits the copula, the demonstrative plus стaло быть supply the link and this noun phrase serves as the conclusion/predicate of the sentence.
segments.p1-5-g128: Что is the interrogative pronoun 'what' beginning the question. Же is an emphatic particle adding force or impatience ('then/indeed'), usually untranslatable as a separate word. Наконец is an adverb 'finally/in the end' and the prepositional phrase в результате ('in result', prepositional case) specifies the semantic scope of the question — together they form a colloquial rhetorical question 'what, ultimately, is the result?'.
segments.p1-5-g129: Да here is an interjection meaning 'yes' or an emphatic particle introducing agreement or exclamation. То же самое is a fixed phrase: то (demonstrative 'that') + же (intensive/contrastive particle) + самое (neuter adjective/noun 'the very one/thing'), together meaning 'the same thing' or 'exactly the same'. The phrase functions as the entire clause/predicate.
segments.p1-5-g130: Вспомните is the imperative plural (or polite) form of 'to remember/recall' used as a direct command or request to the addressee(s). The colon that follows signals the speaker will provide or recall an example.
segments.p1-5-g131: Давеча вот is an adverbial phrase ('just recently/the other day'), with вот functioning as a discourse marker adding immediacy. Я говорил is the past-tense masculine singular of говорить ('I spoke/was speaking'), and о мщении is a prepositional phrase ('about revenge'); the verb governs the prepositional object and the whole segment states when and about what the speaker spoke.
segments.p1-5-g1: 'Ну' is an interjection here meaning 'well' or 'well then'; 'разве' is a question particle making the clause rhetorical ('really?/is it possible?'); 'можно' is the impersonal predicate 'it is possible'. Together they form a rhetorical opening: 'Well, is it possible...'.
segments.p1-5-g2: 'Разве можно' repeats the rhetorical question; 'хоть' + 'сколько-нибудь' (хоть сколько-нибудь) is an indefinite quantifier meaning 'even a little/at all'; 'уважать' is the infinitive 'to respect' and 'себя' is the reflexive 'oneself'; 'человеку' is dative ('for a person' / 'for one') marking who would or would not respect himself. Word order places the impersonal question before the dative to emphasize the moral impossibility.
segments.p1-5-g3: 'Который' begins a relative clause modifying 'человеку' ('a man who...'); 'даже' = 'even'; 'в самом чувстве' = 'in the very feeling' (prepositional phrase with 'самом' as intensifier); 'собственного унижения' = 'of his own humiliation'; 'посягнул' is perfective past of 'посягнуть' meaning 'attempted/encroached upon' and is followed by the infinitive 'отыскать' ('to find/seek'); 'наслаждение' is the direct object 'pleasure'. The clause describes the shocking fact that he tried to find pleasure even in his humiliation.
segments.p1-5-g4: Negative construction 'Я не от ... говорю' = 'I'm not speaking out of/from ...'. 'Приторного' ('cloying'/'sickly') modifies 'раскаянья' ('repentance'); 'какого-нибудь' (какого + нибудь) is an indefinite phrase meaning 'some kind of/any'; 'так теперь говорю' places manner ('так' = 'thus') and time ('теперь' = 'now') after the complement and 'говорю' is the present tense 'I say/speak'.
segments.p1-5-g5: 'Да и вообще' = 'and besides/in general'; 'терпеть ... не мог' is an idiom meaning 'I couldn't bear/tolerate' with 'я не мог' (I could not) plus the infinitive 'говорить' (to say). The word order highlights the speaker's inability to tolerate saying that phrase.
segments.p1-5-g6: Quoted speech begins; 'Простите' is the polite/plural imperative of 'простить' meaning 'forgive (me/us)'. It functions as a direct address in the quote.
segments.p1-5-g7: 'Папаша' is a familiar or ironic vocative for 'father' ('Papa' / 'old man') used here as the addressee in the quoted apology.
segments.p1-5-g8: 'Вперед не буду' is colloquial and here quoted as the apologetic promise; literally 'I will not go forward,' but idiomatically 'I won't do it again.' 'Не буду' is the future tense 'I will not'.
segments.p1-5-g9: Contrast marker: '— не потому,' begins 'not because...' to introduce the reason that follows.
segments.p1-5-g10: 'Чтоб' here functions colloquially as a subordinating conjunction 'that'/'that it was the case that'; 'я не был способен' would be the full phrase, but Dostoevsky places 'не способен был' with past 'был' to mean 'I was not capable'; 'это сказать' is the infinitive phrase 'to say this.' Overall: 'not that I was incapable of saying it.'
segments.p1-5-g11: 'А напротив' = 'but on the contrary' (introduces the opposing explanation). 'А' is the adversative 'but' and 'напротив' reinforces the contrast.
segments.p1-5-g12: 'Может быть' is the fixed phrase 'perhaps/maybe'; grammatically it's composed of the modal 'может' and 'быть' but functions adverbially here to hedge the following statement.
segments.p1-5-g13: 'Именно' = 'precisely/exactly' intensifies 'потому' = 'for that reason'; together 'именно потому' = 'precisely because.'
segments.p1-5-g14: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that') giving the cause: 'уж' is an intensifier ('indeed/very'); 'слишком' = 'too/excessively'; 'способен на это' = 'capable of that' (with 'на' governing the object); 'бывал' is the past habitual 'used to be' or 'had been' and signals repeated past instances — so the clause says he had been all too often capable of saying it.
segments.p1-5-g15: 'Да еще как?' is an exclamatory tag meaning 'and how!' or 'and how indeed!' used to emphasize how strongly or frequently that was the case; 'да' is a colloquial intensifier here, 'еще' = 'even/more', and 'как' = 'how.'
segments.p1-5-g16: 'Как нарочно' is an idiom meaning 'as if on purpose' or 'as though deliberately'; 'и' is a connective 'and/even'; 'влопаюсь' is a colloquial first-person form 'I blunder into / I fall into' (here used with the adverb 'бывало' to describe recurring past events).
segments.p1-5-g17: 'Бывало' is an adverb meaning 'it used to happen' or 'as was often the case,' and with present/future-form verbs it creates a habitual-past nuance ('I would blunder into...').
segments.p1-5-g18: 'В таком случае' is a prepositional phrase 'in such a case' ('в' + locative 'таком' + 'случае'), marking the circumstance in which the blundering occurred.
segments.p1-5-g19: 'Когда' = 'when' introduces the subordinate clause; 'сам' = emphatic 'myself' (i.e., 'I myself'); 'ни сном' is the first half of the idiom 'ни сном, ни духом' meaning 'not in the least / not even by a dream' with 'сном' here 'dream.'
segments.p1-5-g20: The continuation of the idiom 'ни духом' ('nor by spirit/hint') followed by 'не виноват' = 'not guilty / not to blame.' The doubled 'ни ... ни ...' is a standard negative coordination strengthening the denial of guilt or blame.
segments.p1-5-g115: 'Где' is the interrogative 'where' starting a rhetorical question; 'у меня' is the possessive construction literally 'at me' (expressing 'do I have'); 'первоначальные причины' is nominative plural meaning 'initial/original causes' (the copula 'есть' is omitted).
segments.p1-5-g116: 'на которые' is a relative phrase 'on/onto which' governed by the preposition 'на' and referring back to 'причины'; 'я упрусь' is first person singular future (reflexive) of 'упереться' — literally 'I will press/lean against', used figuratively 'I can rely on'.
segments.p1-5-g117: A short clause repeating the question: 'где' = 'where' and 'основания' (nominative plural) = 'foundations' or 'bases'; it's elliptical for 'where are the foundations?'.
segments.p1-5-g118: 'Откуда' = 'from where' (rhetorical); 'я' is the subject; 'их' is the accusative plural pronoun 'them' (referring to the previously mentioned causes); 'возьму' is the perfective future of 'взять' ('I will take/get'), making the question about the source of those causes.
segments.p1-5-g119: 'Я упражняюсь' is present tense imperfective reflexive 'I practise/exercise myself'; 'в мышлении' is the prepositional phrase 'in thinking' (i.e. 'I exercise my mind' or 'I occupy myself with thinking').
segments.p1-5-g120: 'а' here links clauses (roughly 'and' / 'but'); 'следственно' is a formal adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore', introduced as a parenthetical connector and slightly elevated/old-fashioned in tone.
segments.p1-5-g121: 'У меня' again expresses 'in me' / 'for me' (possession or occurrence within); 'всякая первоначальная причина' is the grammatical subject ('every initial cause'); 'тотчас же' is an adverbial phrase 'immediately' with 'же' adding emphasis; 'тащит' is present imperfective 'drags/pulls' and 'за собою' is a reflexive/idiomatic complement 'after itself' or 'along with it'; 'другую' is the accusative 'another' (feminine) referring back to 'причина'.
segments.p1-5-g122: 'еще' here means 'even/more'; 'первоначальнее' is the comparative form of 'первоначальный' ('more original' or 'still more primordial') used elliptically (the noun 'причина' is omitted but implied).
segments.p1-5-g123: 'и так далее' = 'and so on/and so forth'; 'в бесконечность' uses the accusative as a directional complement 'into infinity' / 'to infinity', completing the idea of an endless regress.
segments.p1-5-g124: 'Такова' is a predicative form meaning 'such is' and agrees in gender with 'сущность' (feminine); 'именно' adds emphasis 'precisely/exactly'; 'сущность' is the noun 'essence'; 'всякого сознания и мышления' is a genitive attribute 'of every consciousness and of thinking', so the whole clause states a general truth about the essence of consciousness and thought.
segments.p1-5-g33: Гадко is an impersonal predicate (adverb) meaning 'it is disgusting/awful' and is placed before the infinitive phrase for emphasis. Это всё вспоминать is an infinitive clause ('to recall all this') that functions as the complement of гадко; это всё = 'all this' with the demonstrative before the noun phrase.
segments.p1-5-g34: Да и тогда is a colloquial connective meaning 'and even then' or 'besides, at that time'; гадко было is the impersonal past (гадко + было) meaning 'it was disgusting' (было marks past tense). The clause restates the speaker's feeling in the past.
segments.p1-5-g35: Ведь is a discourse particle ('after all/you see') introducing the remark; через минуту какую-нибудь is a time expression 'in/after a minute or so' with какую-нибудь making it indefinite; я уже со злобою соображаю is present/imperfective 'I already, with malice, realize' — с + instr. злобою marks manner ('with malice'), and соображаю is 1st person singular imperfective.
segments.p1-5-g36: Бывало is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'it would happen' or 'as was often the case' — it softens/frames the following clause as a habitual or recurring reaction.
segments.p1-5-g37: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('that') dependent on соображаю; все это = 'all this' is the noun phrase subject/complement, and ложь is the predicate noun 'a lie'.
segments.p1-5-g38: The repeated ложь is an emphatic repetition (ellipsis of a verb) — a rhetorical insistence on the noun 'lie'.
segments.p1-5-g39: Отвратительная напускная ложь is a stacked adjective phrase modifying ложь: отвратительная 'abhorrent/disgusting' + напускная 'affected/put-on', forming 'an abhorrent, affected lie.'
segments.p1-5-g40: То есть is the idiom 'that is (to say)' introducing a paraphrase or examples; все эти раскаяния lists one example ('all those repentances') and functions as an apposition to 'lie'.
segments.p1-5-g41: Another item in the enumerated paraphrase: все эти умиления = 'all those sentimental/tender feelings' (умиления is plural noun). The phrase continues the list introduced by то есть.
segments.p1-5-g42: Final item of the list: все эти обеты возрождения = 'all those vows of rebirth/renewal'; возрождения is in the genitive, modifying обеты ('vows of...').
segments.p1-5-g43: А here is a simple connective ('and/but') introducing the imperative; спросите is the 2nd-person plural (or formal) imperative 'ask' (addressing the reader/listener to pose the question).
segments.p1-5-g44: Для чего = 'for what purpose/why' (asks the reason). Я так сам себя коверкал и мучил is a past narrative: коверкал и мучил are imperfective past-tense verbs ('I mangled/distorted and tormented') with сам себя 'myself' (emphatic reflexive accusative). Так modifies the manner ('so/that way').
segments.p1-5-g45: Ответ: is a heading-like noun plus colon ('Answer:') introducing the speaker's reply.
segments.p1-5-g46: Затем here functions causally ('for that reason' / 'because') and leads into the explanatory clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-5-g47: Что introduces the content of the answer ('that...'). Скучно уж очень было is an impersonal past construction meaning 'it was dreadfully/very boring' (скучно + было, уж + очень intensify). Сложа руки сидеть is a gerundial construction (сложа руки = 'with folded hands') + infinitive сидеть 'to sit', functioning as the activity that was boring.
segments.p1-5-g48: Вот и is an idiomatic resultive connective meaning 'and so/thereupon', introducing the consequence. Пускался is past imperfective reflexive ('I took to/started doing'), and на выверты literally 'to tricks/twists' — i.e. 'I set about tricks' or 'I resorted to contrivances.'
segments.p1-5-g72: "Меня как-то всю жизнь тянуло такие штуки выкидывать," is an impersonal past construction: "меня тянуло" literally 'me was pulling' functions as 'I felt drawn'; "как-то" is an indefinite adverb 'somehow'; "всю жизнь" is a time adverbial 'all my life'; the infinitive clause "такие штуки выкидывать" is the object of the feeling verb and uses the imperfective infinitive to express a habitual/recurring tendency ('to do such things/stunts').
segments.p1-5-g73: "так что уж я стал под конец и в себе не властен." — "так что" is a result clause marker ('so that'); "уж" here is an emphatic particle ('indeed/already'); "я стал" is past of "стать" ('I became/ended up'); "под конец" is an adverbial phrase 'towards the end / in the end'; "и" here adds the sense 'even/also'; "в себе не властен" is a predicative adjective construction meaning 'not master/sovereign over myself' (в себе = 'over myself').
segments.p1-5-g74: "Другой раз" is an adverbial phrase meaning 'on another occasion' or 'one other time'; the infinitive "влюбиться" ('to fall in love') precedes the finite verb for emphasis, and "насильно" ('by force') modifies that infinitive; "захотел" is past tense 'I wanted' (implied subject я), so the whole clause means 'once I wanted to force myself to fall in love.'
segments.p1-5-g75: "даже два раза." is an afterthought intensifier: "даже" = 'even', and "два раза" = 'two times' (numeral + noun — note the genitive singular form "раза" after the numeral two), specifying frequency.
segments.p1-5-g76: Страдал is the past tense, masculine singular form of страдать; the subject (я) is omitted and understood. Ведь is a particle adding emphasis/confirmation ('after all' / 'you know') placed after the verb for colloquial force.
segments.p1-5-g77: Господа is a vocative-like address (plural) meaning 'gentlemen' and stands alone as the addressee; punctuation sets it off.
segments.p1-5-g78: Уверяю is 1st person singular present of уверять ('I assure'); вас is the accusative pronoun 'you' (object). Together they reinforce the preceding claim.
segments.p1-5-g79: В глубине (в + noun in locative) = 'in the depth'; the enclitic-то (глубине-то) is an emphatic particle highlighting 'deep down.' Души is genitive ('of the soul' / 'of the heart') modifying глубине. Не верится is an impersonal construction of вериться meaning 'it doesn't seem believable' (literally 'is not believed').
segments.p1-5-g80: Что introduces a content clause 'that…'. Страдаешь is 2nd person singular present of страдать; here it functions as the predicate inside the subordinate clause ('that you/one am/is suffering'), a colloquial rhetorical use that can refer back to the speaker.
segments.p1-5-g81: Насмешка is the nominative subject 'a mockery' or 'a sneer.' Шевелится is the 3rd person singular present of шевелиться (reflexive), 'stirs' or 'moves slightly,' personifying the mockery as stirring within.
segments.p1-5-g82: А is a contrastive conjunction 'but' or 'and whereas.' Все-таки is a compound adverb ('nevertheless'/'all the same'); here it is split into two words in the annotation. Страдаю is 1st person present 'I suffer,' asserting the speaker's experience despite the preceding doubt.
segments.p1-5-g83: Да еще is an emphatic combination: да here functions like 'and/indeed' (colloquial intensifier), ещё = 'even/more' adding emphasis. Настоящим is an adjective in instrumental (implying an omitted noun, e.g. 'suffering') used nominally to mean 'a real/genuine one.'
segments.p1-5-g84: Заправским образом is an adverbial phrase in instrumental: заправским (colloquial adjective 'thorough/genuine') + образом ('manner/way'), together 'in a thoroughly genuine way.' The phrase modifies how the suffering occurs.
segments.p1-5-g85: РевнУю is 1st person singular present of ревновать 'I am jealous'; it is a short independent clause stating the emotion.
segments.p1-5-g86: Из себя is a fixed reflexive phrase 'out of oneself' (из + genitive), and выхожу is 1st person singular present of выходить 'I go out/leave'; together they form the idiom 'I go out of myself,' meaning 'I lose my composure / am beside myself.'
segments.p1-5-g49: Право here is an interjection/particle meaning 'indeed' or 'really' used to strengthen agreement; it stands alone and is followed by a comma for emphasis. It is colloquial/old-fashioned but common in Dostoevsky's style.
segments.p1-5-g50: Так is an adverb meaning 'so' or 'thus' and here affirms the preceding statement ('that is so'). It functions as a comment on the whole utterance rather than modifying a specific verb.
segments.p1-5-g51: Замечайте is an imperative (2nd person plural or polite form) formed from the imperfective замечать, urging the addressees to 'notice' or 'pay attention'. Получше is a colloquial comparative/adverb meaning 'more/better' and modifies the verb; сами за собой is a reflexive phrase meaning 'yourselves (with regard to your own behavior)', where за собой functions as a reflexive prepositional phrase 'about/with respect to oneself.'
segments.p1-5-g52: Господа is a vocative plural addressing the audience — 'gentlemen' or 'sirs' — and here it simply marks who is being spoken to; punctuation (comma) shows it is a direct address.
segments.p1-5-g53: Тогда is an adverb 'then / in that case' setting the time/condition for the result. И here adds slight emphasis ('and/even'), and поймете is future tense (2nd person plural) of понять/поймёте meaning 'you will understand' — the main result promised if they follow the imperative.
segments.p1-5-g54: Что introduces a subordinate clause functioning as the object of поймёте ('that ...'). Это is the demonstrative 'this/it', and так repeats the idea 'so/true' — together: 'that this is so.'
segments.p1-5-g55: Сам себе is a reflexive/dative pair meaning 'to/for myself' with сам adding emphasis ('myself'). Приключения выдумывал is past imperfective (1st person singular) meaning 'I would invent/used to invent adventures' — выдумывал is habitual/past narration. И жизнь сочинял is a coordinate verb phrase ('and I made up a life'), with сочинял parallel in tense and aspect.
segments.p1-5-g56: Чтоб introduces a purpose clause ('so that / in order that'). Хоть как-нибудь is a colloquial softening phrase meaning 'at least somehow' or 'somehow, anyhow' (хоть = 'at least/even', как-нибудь = 'in some way'). Да here is a colloquial particle linking to the infinitive and adds a purposive or concessive flavor, and пожить is an imperfective/perfective infinitive meaning 'to live (a bit)/to have some life.' Together: 'in order to live somehow, at least.'
segments.p1-5-g57: Сколько раз мне случалось is an impersonal/past-habitual construction: сколько раз 'how many times', мне is the dative marking the experiencer ('to me'), and случалось is the neuter past/imperfective meaning 'it happened / used to happen.' Ну is a colloquial interjection ('well') introducing hesitation or an example.
segments.p1-5-g58: Хоть here is a particle meaning 'even' or 'for example' used to introduce an example or concessive instance; it softens or highlights what follows.
segments.p1-5-g59: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example', signaling that the following infinitive is an illustration of what happened.
segments.p1-5-g60: Обижаться is an infinitive used nominally to name the example activity: 'to take offense / to be offended.' Grammatically it follows the parenthetical and names the action that used to occur.
segments.p1-5-g61: Так is a discourse particle here, roughly 'so' or 'like that', marking the manner or tone of the example and linking back to the speaker's anecdotal style.
segments.p1-5-g62: Не из-за чего is an idiomatic chunk meaning 'for no reason' or 'there was nothing to be offended about'. It combines the negation не with the prepositional phrase из‑за чего ('because of what'), so together 'not because of anything.'
segments.p1-5-g63: Нарочно is an adverb meaning 'deliberately' or 'on purpose' and here intensifies the oddity: taking offense as if intentionally or putting on an air.
segments.p1-5-g64: И ведь сам знаешь is a conversational tag: и (and), ведь (after all/you know), сам ('yourself', emphatic), знаешь ('you know' — 2nd person present). Together: 'and, you know yourself,' calling the listener's attention to the shared recognition.
segments.p1-5-g65: Бывало is an adverbial/past-tense adverb meaning 'it would happen' or 'there used to be occasions' — used to introduce a habitual past occurrence ('it happened that...').
segments.p1-5-g66: Что introduces the subordinate clause ('that'); не из-за чего обиделся repeats the idiom 'was offended for no reason' (не = not; из-за чего = because of anything; обиделся = reflexive past 'became offended'). This clause reports a past occurrence.
segments.p1-5-g67: Напустил на себя is a perfective past verb phrase where напустил (from напустить) here means 'put on' (a mood/affectation) and на себя ('onto oneself') indicates the reflexive target: 'I put on [an affect/air] on myself.' It's idiomatic: 'напустить на себя' = affect oneself with something.
segments.p1-5-g68: Но до того себя доведешь is a general/predictive statement: но ('but'), до того ('to such an extent' — до + demonstrative того), себя ('yourself'), доведёшь (2nd person singular future of довести 'you will bring/drive (yourself)') — meaning 'but you drive yourself to such a point.' The 2nd-person form is used generically (you/one).
segments.p1-5-g69: Что introduces the result clause ('that'), and под конец is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'towards the end / in the end / eventually'. Конец is the noun 'end'; под here conveys 'towards/near' in this phrase.
segments.p1-5-g70: Право is used again as an interjection/particle meaning 'really' or 'honestly' — a colloquial intensifier before the final result.
segments.p1-5-g71: И в самом деле is a fixed phrase meaning 'and in fact / and really' (в + самый + дело), where самом and деле form the idiom. Обидишься is future 2nd person singular reflexive 'you will get offended' (used here as a general rhetorical 'you/one will end up offended').
segments.p1-5-g89: 'И' is a connective particle here ('and' / linking to what came before). 'все' is an impersonal/neutral pronoun meaning 'everything' or 'it's all' and functions as the subject/topic of the clause; 'от скуки' is a prepositional phrase with 'от' + genitive ('скуки') meaning 'from/because of boredom'. The whole phrase = 'and it's all because of boredom.'
segments.p1-5-g90: This single word is a direct address (vocative): 'господа' = 'gentlemen' / 'sirs' (here used to address the reader/listeners). It is set off by commas and does not alter the clause's grammar.
segments.p1-5-g91: A repetition of the earlier clause for emphasis: 'все от скуки' again = 'all because of boredom.' The semicolon after it prepares the following short independent clause.
segments.p1-5-g92: 'инерция' is the feminine noun subject (nominative). 'задавила' is the perfective past tense, 3rd person singular (feminine), meaning literally 'crushed' or figuratively 'overwhelmed/suppressed' — the verb agrees with инерция and completes the sentence: 'inertia crushed (us/it).' The object is implied rather than explicit.
segments.p1-5-g93: 'Ведь' is a discourse particle often rendered 'after all' or 'you see', introducing an explanation. 'прямой' is the first adjective in a series modifying the noun 'плод' later; here it means 'direct' or 'straightforward.'
segments.p1-5-g94: 'законный' is the second adjective in the series modifying 'плод' and means 'legitimate' or 'rightful' (not 'legal' in a bureaucratic sense, but 'proper/legitimate' as a result). It continues the descriptive string before the noun.
segments.p1-5-g95: This is the noun phrase head and the equative clause: 'непосредственный плод сознания' = 'the immediate/ direct fruit/result of consciousness' (непосредственный modifying плод; сознания is genitive showing possession/source). The dash plus 'это' marks an equative clause: 'this is inertia.' 'это' here functions as a demonstrative/copular element linking subject and predicate.
segments.p1-5-g96: 'то есть' is the fixed phrase 'that is' / 'that is to say' introducing a gloss. 'сознательное' is an adjective meaning 'conscious' or 'deliberate'. 'сложа' is a de‑participial form (деепричастие) from 'сложить' meaning 'having folded' and functions adverbially to describe the manner of 'сиденье' — literally 'having folded (the) hands'. The hyphenated compound 'сложа-руки-сиденье' is a stylistic nominal phrase meaning 'the sitting with folded hands' (i.e., deliberate inaction). 'есть' here is the verb 'to be' within the phrase 'то есть'.
segments.p1-5-g97: Full clause: 'Я' (I) is the subject. 'уж' is a particle often meaning 'already' (adds emphasis that the action is not new). 'об этом' ('об' + locative 'этом') is a prepositional phrase 'about this.' 'упоминал' is past tense imperfective 'I mentioned / I have mentioned' (imperfective used to note that the action occurred, without focusing on completion). 'выше' is an adverb meaning 'above' or 'earlier in the text'; together the clause says 'I already mentioned this above.'
segments.p1-5-g98: 'Повторяю' is the present-tense first-person singular of 'повторять' — 'I repeat.' As a single-word sentence it functions as a stage direction/authorial aside emphasizing the act of repetition.
segments.p1-5-g99: 'усиленно' is an adverb meaning 'intensely' or 'forcefully' modifying the following verb. The repeated 'повторяю' (present 1st person) intensifies the act: 'I repeat forcefully.' The colon introduces the content being emphasized.
segments.p1-5-g100: This is the main clause's subject phrase: 'все непосредственные люди и деятели' = 'all immediate/direct people and doers/men of action' (все = 'all', непосредственные = 'direct/immediate', люди = 'people/individuals', деятели = 'active persons/men of action'). 'потому и деятельны' follows (see next segment) — 'потому' ('for that reason') links to the subordinate clause introduced by 'что'.
segments.p1-5-g101: 'что' introduces the subordinate explanatory clause 'that ...'. 'они' is the clause subject 'they'. 'тупы' and 'ограничены' are short-form predicates (plural) describing the subject: 'they are dull' (тупы) and 'limited/confined' (ограничены). Grammatically, this subordinate clause gives the reason why the people described are active.
segments.p1-5-g132: Вы is the second-person pronoun used here as the polite/formal 'you' (capitalized in original). In spoken/written Russian addressed to one person respectfully, Вы still takes plural agreement (see the verb).
segments.p1-5-g133: верно is an adverbial parenthetical meaning 'surely' or 'probably' used to soften or hedge the statement; it modifies the whole clause that follows and is placed between commas.
segments.p1-5-g134: не is the standard negation particle. вникли is the past tense, perfective form (perfective verb вникнуть) in the plural past form; with Вы it expresses polite second-person past ('you did not penetrate/understand/attend to the point'). Word order places negation before the verb as usual.
segments.p1-5-g135: Сказано is a short passive/past participle used impersonally: 'it is said' / 'they say' / 'it has been said'. As a sentence-initial remark it functions like 'they say' or 'it is said'.
segments.p1-5-g136: человек is a generic noun 'a person/man' in the nominative; мстит is present tense, 3rd person singular of мстить ('takes revenge'/'avenges'), making a general statement about people. The small clause expresses a habitual/general truth.
segments.p1-5-g137: потому is an adverb meaning 'for that reason' and together with the following что forms the causal conjunction 'потому что' = 'because'. находит is present tense, 3rd person singular of находить ('finds/considers'); в этом is the preposition в + demonstrative pronoun in the prepositional case meaning 'in this'; справедливость is the direct object meaning 'justice' or 'a sense of justice'.
segments.p1-5-g138: Значит here is a discourse particle meaning 'so' or 'therefore' (literally from the verb 'to mean/signify') and introduces the consequence or summary that follows.
segments.p1-5-g139: он is the nominative pronoun 'he'; первоначальную is the feminine accusative singular form of первоначальный modifying причину; причину is the feminine accusative singular of 'причина' (direct object 'the cause'); нашёл is past tense, masculine singular of найти ('found'). Word order places the adjective before the noun and the verb after — a neutral Russian order for 'he found the original cause.'
segments.p1-5-g140: основание (accusative singular) is a second direct object 'basis' or 'ground'; нашёл is repeated for emphasis — same past-tense verb as before. The repetition lists two synonymous objects he 'found.'
segments.p1-5-g141: а is a coordinating particle here functioning like 'and'/'but' introducing a specification; именно is an adverb meaning 'namely' or 'that is to say', preparing a clarification or specification that follows.
segments.p1-5-g142: справедливость is the noun 'justice/fairness' in the nominative used here as the specification of what he found — the clarifying noun after 'а именно'.
segments.p1-5-g143: Стало быть is a fixed phrase meaning 'therefore' or 'it follows that' built from the past neuter of стать (стало) plus быть; here it functions as a discourse connective introducing the logical conclusion.
segments.p1-5-g144: он is the subject 'he'; со всех сторон is a prepositional/adverbial phrase literally 'from all sides' (со is a variant of с used before certain clusters, всех is genitive plural of весь 'all', сторон is genitive plural of сторона 'side'); успокоен is a short passive/adjectival participle meaning 'calm/settled' or 'reassured' — predicate in short-form agrees with the subject in meaning but not inflection.
segments.p1-5-g145: а is the coordinating conjunction connecting clauses ('and/so/whereas'); следственно is an adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'as a result', signaling the consequence about to be stated.
segments.p1-5-g146: и is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' / 'also'; отмщает is present tense, 3rd person singular of отмщать/отмстить meaning 'takes revenge' or 'avenges'; спокойно and успешно are adverbial modifiers 'calmly' and 'successfully' respectively, the first placed immediately after the verb and the second linked by и for emphasis.
segments.p1-5-g147: будучи is a gerund (деепричастие) formed from быть meaning 'being' and introduces a circumstantial clause; убеждён is a short adjective/participle meaning 'convinced' — together: 'being convinced'. This gerundial clause gives the reason or mental state accompanying his action.
segments.p1-5-g148: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause; делает is present tense, 3rd person singular of делать 'does' or 'is doing'; честное and справедливое are adjective nominative/accusative neuter singular forms modifying дело; дело here means 'deed/affair' or 'cause' — 'an honest and just deed/undertaking.' The adjective-noun sequence is the direct object of делает.
segments.p1-5-g102: This is a short rhetorical question built around the infinitive. Как here is the interrogative 'how'; это is the direct object pronoun placed between question word and the infinitive (normal Russian word order in such constructions); объяснить is the infinitive 'to explain' (no tense) — together: 'how to explain this?'.
segments.p1-5-g103: А вот как: is a discourse marker introducing an explanation: А is a connective/contrastive particle ('and'/'well'/'but' depending on tone), вот is a demonstrative/emphatic particle ('here/now'), and как is 'how'. The colon marks that an explanation follows.
segments.p1-5-g104: This clause gives the cause and uses a prepositional causal phrase + noun phrase + phrasal verb: они is the subject 'they'; вследствие (preposition) + своей ограниченности (genitive) = 'because of their limitation/narrowness'; ближайшие и второстепенные причины is the direct object (accusative plural) 'immediate and secondary causes'; за + accusative (первоначальные) with принимать forms the lexicalized verb phrase принимать за 'to take/assume X for Y'. The verb принимают is present, 3rd person plural, agreeing with они.
segments.p1-5-g105: Таким образом is an adverbial phrase 'in this way/thus' linking to the previous clause; скорее и легче are comparative adverbs 'more quickly/sooner and more easily' modifying убеждаются; других is genitive plural used in the comparative sense 'than others'; убеждаются is the reflexive verb 'become convinced' (3rd person plural present). Word order places the adverbial and comparatives before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p1-5-g106: This is a subordinate clause introduced by что 'that'. Непреложное (neuter nominative) modifies основание (nominative) = 'an unalterable/inviolable foundation'. Своему делу is in the dative ('for their cause/for their affair') showing the beneficiary/target of the foundation. Нашли is the past plural of найти ('they found'/'they have found') and agrees with the earlier subject они; together: 'that they have found an unassailable foundation for their cause.'
segments.p1-5-g107: Ну и is a colloquial connective meaning roughly 'and so' or 'well then' introducing the result; успокоиваются is the present reflexive verb 'they calm down'/'they become calm' (3rd person plural). The whole phrase expresses the result of their conviction.
segments.p1-5-g108: А ведь is a contrastive/emphatic combination ('and/yet, after all'); это is the demonstrative pronoun serving as the subject/predicate introducer; главное is an adjective used substantively as the predicate noun 'the main thing' (neuter nominative). The clause asserts that this consequence is the principal point.
segments.p1-5-g109: Ведь is an emphatic particle 'after all'; чтоб here is the colloquial form of чтобы and introduces a purpose/infinitive-clause; начать is the perfective infinitive 'to begin', and действовать is the infinitive 'to act' — the sequence начать + infinitive = 'to begin to act'. The phrase sets up the condition/purpose for what follows.
segments.p1-5-g110: Нужно is an impersonal predicative meaning 'it is necessary'; быть is the infinitive 'to be'; совершенно is an adverb 'completely'; успокоенным is a passive/participial adjective in the instrumental (complement of быть) meaning 'calmed'/'settled'; предварительно is an adverb 'in advance'. The construction нужно быть + [adjective/participle] expresses necessity: 'one must be completely calmed beforehand.'
segments.p1-5-g111: И connects the second requirement; чтоб again introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that'); сомнений is genitive plural 'of doubts' (the partitive/genitive after оставаться/не оставаться); уж is an emphatic particle ('already/at all' here strengthening the negation); никаких is the negative determiner in genitive plural 'no/none'; не оставалось is the impersonal/past-form verb 'would remain'/'be left' — with чтоб + past-form used here to express the intended result/subjunctive-like sense: 'so that there should be no doubts left.'
segments.p1-5-g112: Ну а как я, is a colloquial opening to a rhetorical question: Ну 'well' is an interjection, а is a contrastive particle often rendered 'but'/'and', как is the interrogative 'how', and я is the subject 'I'. The comma marks a pause before the parenthetical например.
segments.p1-5-g113: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' inserted to make the question concrete or illustrative; it does not change the core grammar of the question.
segments.p1-5-g114: Себя is the reflexive accusative pronoun 'myself' placed before the verb (stylistic/inverted order instead of успокоюсь); успокою is the first person singular future of the perfective verb успокоить 'I will calm' (here used with себя to mean 'calm myself'). The clause is a direct question about how the speaker could accomplish self-calming.
segments.p1-5-g199: "Но" is the adversative conjunction 'but'; the phrase "что же делать" is an impersonal interrogative idiom meaning 'what is one to do' or 'what can be done'. The particle "же" is emphatic and strengthens the question; "делать" is the infinitive used impersonally (no explicit subject).
segments.p1-5-g200: "Если" introduces a conditional clause ('if'). "Прямое и единственное назначение" is a noun phrase in the neuter nominative (both adjectives agree with "назначение") meaning 'the direct and sole purpose'. "Всякого умного человека" is a genitive attribute ('of every intelligent person') modifying "назначение"; Russian often places the possessor/attribute after the head noun. "Есть" here is the present copula ('is'), and "болтовня" is the predicate noun 'chatter'.
segments.p1-5-g201: "То есть" is a fixed connective meaning 'that is' / 'that is to say', used to restate or clarify. "Умышленное пересыпанье" is a nominal phrase ("умышленное" agrees in gender/number with the neuter noun "пересыпанье") meaning 'a deliberate pouring/shifting' (a verbal noun from пересыпать). The prepositional phrase "из пустого в порожнее" is literal 'from the empty into the vain'; "из" governs genitive ("пустого"), while "в" here governs the accusative/target noun ("порожнее") indicating direction/metaphorical movement from one empty thing to another.
segments.p1-5-g149: А ведь is a combination of contrastive particle а ('but/and') plus the emphatic particle ведь ('after all'), used to introduce a contrary fact. The core clause is я (subject) + справедливости (object in the genitive because of negation) + тут (adverb 'here') + не вижу (present tense, 1st person singular 'I do not see').
segments.p1-5-g150: добродетели тоже никакой не нахожу: object (добродетели) again appears with negation (никакой не нахожу) — Russian often uses a form like this to mean 'I find no virtue.' Тоже ('also') links this negative observation to the previous one. Никакой is an attributive negating pronoun modifying the noun; нахожу is present tense 'I find.'
segments.p1-5-g151: а следственно: а is a linking/contrasting particle ('and/but'), and следственно is an adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore', introducing the logical result of the preceding clauses.
segments.p1-5-g152: если стану мстить: если 'if' introduces a conditional clause; стану is the 1st person singular future of стать used with an infinitive to mean 'I begin to / I should begin to' (here hypothetical: 'if I were to begin to'); мстить is the infinitive 'to take revenge/retaliate.'
segments.p1-5-g153: то разве только из злости: то is the correlative particle of the conditional ('then/in that case'); разве только is an idiomatic combination meaning 'only/merely' (a mild, somewhat rhetorical limiter); из + genitive (злости) means 'out of' or 'from' (indicating motive: 'out of anger').
segments.p1-5-g154: Злость is the nominative noun placed at the front for emphasis and functions as the subject of the clause ('Anger').
segments.p1-5-g155: конечно is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'certainly' and modifies the whole statement, marking it as the speaker's qualification.
segments.p1-5-g156: могла бы все пересилить: могла бы is the past form of мочь plus the particle бы to form a conditional/potential ('could have' / 'might have'); все is the direct object 'everything'; пересилить is the perfective infinitive 'to overpower/overcome' — together: 'could have overcome everything.'
segments.p1-5-g157: все мои сомнения: a noun phrase where все ('all') modifies мои ('my') and сомнения ('doubts'); here it specifies what 'everything' refers to: 'all my doubts.'
segments.p1-5-g158: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and', linking this clause to the concluding phrase that follows.
segments.p1-5-g159: стало быть is a fixed colloquial phrase (literally 'having become to be') meaning 'therefore' or 'so' and serves to sum up or draw a conclusion from what preceded.
segments.p1-5-g160: могла бы совершенно успешно послужить вместо первоначальной причины именно потому: могла бы + infinitive again expresses conditional/potential ('could have served'); совершенно успешно are adverbial modifiers 'completely/successfully'; послужить is the infinitive 'to serve as'; вместо + genitive (первоначальной причины) means 'in place of the original cause'; именно потому is an emphatic link 'precisely for the reason (that)...' preparing the following subordinate clause.
segments.p1-5-g161: что она не причина: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the reason clause; она is the pronoun 'it/she' referring back to 'it' (злость); не причина is the predicate 'is not a cause' (negated nominal predicate in nominative, no article in Russian).
segments.p1-5-g162: Но что же делать is a rhetorical question: Но ('but') + что же ('what then' with the emphatic particle же) + infinitive делать ('to do') — a common idiom meaning 'But what is one to do?' or 'But what am I to do?'.
segments.p1-5-g163: если у меня и злости нет (...) : если 'if' introduces the condition; у меня indicates possession ('I have') with the genitive following нет; the particle и before злости gives the sense 'not even' (intensifier); злости (genitive) is used because нет governs genitive; нет = 'there is no'. The parenthetical (я давеча ведь с этого и начал) = 'I, just the other day, after all, began from this' — давеча 'recently/the other day', ведь 'after all', с этого 'from this', и начал is colloquial past 'and/indeed began' meaning 'this is what I started from'.
segments.p1-5-g182: Послезавтра is an adverb meaning 'the day after tomorrow' placed sentence-initially to set the time; the comma marks it off as an introductory time phrase.
segments.p1-5-g183: Это уж самый поздний срок is a parenthetical demonstrative statement: это ('this/that') + уж (an emphatic particle 'already/indeed') + самый (superlative marker 'the very/most') + поздний срок ('late deadline/time'); together it means 'this is already the latest deadline' and qualifies the time given in the previous segment.
segments.p1-5-g184: Самого себя презирать начнешь uses самого себя (emphatic reflexive 'oneself' in accusative to stress 'your very self') + презирать ('to despise') and начнешь (future tense, 2nd person singular, perfective 'you will begin'); за то ('for that/for the fact') prepares the following subordinate clause that gives the reason.
segments.p1-5-g185: Что introduces the reason-clause ('that...'); самого себя again emphasizes the reflexive object; зазнамо is an archaic/adverbial word meaning 'arrogantly' modifying the verb; надул is past tense, masculine singular perfective 'duped/cheated', describing a completed action (so: 'for having arrogantly duped yourself').
segments.p1-5-g186: В результате is a fixed adverbial phrase formed with the preposition в + noun in the prepositional (or set) form, functioning here as an introductory formula meaning 'as a result' or 'the result is'.
segments.p1-5-g187: Мыльный пузырь и инерция is a nominal list in nominative: мыльный пузырь ('soap bubble'—literal but used figuratively as a fragile illusion) + и ('and') + инерция ('inertia', here psychological/behavioral resistance to change); this names the outcome announced by the previous segment.
segments.p1-5-g188: О господа is an address/exclamatory opening: О is the interjection 'O' (here listed under 'о') and господа is the vocative/plural form used to address 'gentlemen'—a rhetorical, somewhat old-fashioned opening.
segments.p1-5-g189: Ведь я is a small connective: ведь ('after all/you see') gives an emphatic, justificatory tone, followed by the subject pronoun я ('I').
segments.p1-5-g190: Может is a parenthetical modal 'maybe' (colloquial), expressing possibility or uncertainty about the statement that follows.
segments.p1-5-g191: Потому только и считаю себя за умного человека combines потому ('for that reason' / 'that's why') + только ('only/merely') + и (here part of the idiomatic 'только и' construction adding emphasis) + считаю себя ('I consider myself') + за (in the collocation считать за = 'to consider as') + умного человека ('a clever person' in accusative). Together: 'that is perhaps the only reason I consider myself clever.'
segments.p1-5-g192: Что всю жизнь ничего не мог ни начать is a subordinate clause introduced by что ('that') with всю жизнь ('all my life') as a temporal adverbial; ничего не мог ('was able to do nothing' = 'could not') uses past tense мог referring to the speaker, and the construction ни начать links to the next segment (correlative ni...ni) to say he could neither begin...
segments.p1-5-g193: Ни окончить completes the correlative negative pair 'ни...ни' ('neither...nor') with окончить as the second infinitive 'to finish' (perfective infinitive), parallel to начать in the previous segment.
segments.p1-5-g194: Пусть as a standalone here is an interjection/particle 'let it be' or 'all right' introducing concession; it can be followed by a repeat to concede the point.
segments.p1-5-g195: Пусть я болтун uses the particle пусть in its hortative/imperative sense 'let (me) ...' followed by the subject я and predicate болтун (nominative) to mean 'let me be a chatterer' (a concessive admission).
segments.p1-5-g196: Безвредный is an adjective in nominative masculine singular modifying the implied noun 'болтун' that follows; it means 'harmless' and functions as the first descriptive apposition.
segments.p1-5-g197: Досадный болтун is a second appositive phrase ('annoying/irksome chatterer') repeating болтун for emphasis; досадный is an attributive adjective agreeing in case, number, and gender.
segments.p1-5-g198: Как и все мы is a comparative/simile: как и ('as/like') + все мы ('all of us'), meaning 'like all of us'—a self-inclusive generalization; и adds the sense 'as well/too' in this fixed phrase.
segments.p1-5-g164: The phrase is arranged like English 'My malice ... is subjected to ...'. 'Злоба у меня' literally 'malice at me' functions as a possessive/locative ('my malice' / 'the malice in me'); 'опять-таки' is an emphatic adverb meaning 'once again/again nevertheless'. 'вследствие этих проклятых законов сознания' is a prepositional phrase ('due to these cursed laws of consciousness') in which 'законов сознания' is a genitive chain ('laws of consciousness'). The verb 'подвергается' is the present passive/reflexive (подвергаться) and takes a dative object ('химическому разложению') to mean 'is subjected to/undergoes chemical decomposition'.
segments.p1-5-g165: 'Смотришь' is a colloquial second-person present form used impersonally ('you see' / 'one sees' / 'you watch'); the dash signals a pithy sequence. 'Предмет' is the subject ('the object/thing'), and 'улетучивается' is a reflexive/intransitive verb ('улетучиваться') meaning 'evaporates' or 'vanishes'.
segments.p1-5-g166: A short clause with plural subject 'резоны' (colloquial for 'reasons' or 'arguments') and the present passive/impersonal verb 'испаряются' meaning 'evaporate/vanish' or 'disappear'. The tone is conversational and accumulative.
segments.p1-5-g167: 'Виновник' (the culprit) is the grammatical subject; 'не отыскивается' is the reflexive/passive present form of 'отыскивать/отыскаться' meaning 'is not found' or 'cannot be located' (impersonal passive sense).
segments.p1-5-g168: This clause uses 'становится' (to become) with the predicate in the instrumental case: 'обида становится не обидой' literally 'the offense/grievance becomes not an offense' — i.e. the resentment stops being a personal affront. The instrumental (обидой) marks the new status or role.
segments.p1-5-g169: 'А' is a contrastive conjunction ('but/and'), and 'фатумом' is in the instrumental case continuing the predication of 'становится' — 'becomes fate/doom.' The instrumental indicates transformation into that condition.
segments.p1-5-g170: The phrase 'чем-то вроде зубной боли' is idiomatic: 'чем-то вроде' means 'something like' and introduces a comparison; 'зубной боли' ('toothache') is in the genitive after 'вроде' and describes the kind of thing the grievance becomes (a blind, impersonal pain).
segments.p1-5-g171: Relative clause modifying 'зубной боли': 'в которой' ('in which') uses the preposition 'в' plus the relative pronoun 'которой' (feminine singular to agree with 'боли'); 'никто не виноват' literally 'no one is guilty' — 'в которой никто не виноват' = 'in which nobody is to blame.'
segments.p1-5-g172: 'А следовательно' is a discourse connective: 'and/so, consequently' introducing the logical consequence that follows from the previous observations.
segments.p1-5-g173: 'Остаётся' ('remains') is impersonal/passive-like meaning 'what remains/is left is ...'. 'Опять-таки' is emphatic 'once again/again,' and the string 'тот же самый выход' ('the very same way out / the same old solution') uses demonstrative and emphatic particles ('тот же самый') to stress sameness. The explanatory 'то есть' ('that is') introduces the concrete expression 'стену побольнее прибить' — literally 'to fix/strike the wall more painfully'; here 'прибить' is used in a colloquial, violent sense ('to beat/pound/strike'), so the clause means to give the wall a harder pounding (figuratively: an aggressive outlet).
segments.p1-5-g174: Ну is an interjection 'well'; и adds a colloquial connective emphasis. Рукой is instrumental 'with the hand' ('by/with a wave of the hand'). Махнешь is 2nd person singular future (perfective махнуть) 'you will wave' and idiomatically 'you'll brush it off/dismiss it.'
segments.p1-5-g175: Потому что is the subordinating conjunction 'because.' Не нашёл is past tense masculine singular with negation 'didn't find' (the past form here matches the implied addressed subject); первоначальной причины is genitive ('of an initial/original cause/reason') — genitive is idiomatic after negated transitive verbs in Russian.
segments.p1-5-g176: 'А' is a connective 'but/and'; 'попробуй' is an imperative ('try'), and 'увлекись' is another imperative (perfective reflexive 'увлечься' — 'become absorbed/get carried away'). Together they form the exhortation 'try to get carried away.' 'Своим чувством' uses the instrumental ('by/with your feeling'), and 'слепо' is an adverb ('blindly').
segments.p1-5-g177: 'Без' governs the genitive: 'без рассуждений' = 'without reasoning/thoughts.' It's a simple prepositional negative construction.
segments.p1-5-g178: Same pattern: 'без первоначальной причины' = 'without an initial/original cause' (genitive after 'без'); 'первоначальной' modifies 'причины' in the genitive singular.
segments.p1-5-g179: 'Отгоняя' is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) from 'отгонять' meaning 'driving away' and expresses a simultaneous action: 'driving away consciousness.' 'Хоть на это время' is a colloquial concessive/local expression meaning 'even/at least for this time' ('хоть' = 'at least/even', 'на это время' = 'for that time').
segments.p1-5-g180: Two imperatives offering alternatives: 'возненавидь' (become hateful/come to hate) or 'полюби' (come to love). The structure is rhetorical: do one or the other so you are not idle.
segments.p1-5-g181: 'Чтоб' introduces purpose ('so that' / 'in order that'), and 'только не сидеть сложа руки' is an idiom: 'just don't sit with your hands folded' — 'сидеть' in infinitive with negation and 'сложа руки' (adverbial participle from 'сложить') meaning 'with folded hands' (i.e., idle). The whole clause expresses the aim: do anything rather than remain idle.

sentences.p1-5-s6: At the same time I, once again, was moved in my soul, repented, shed tears and, of course, puffed myself up, although I really didn't pretend at all.
sentences.p1-5-s7: My heart was already behaving nastily at that point.
sentences.p1-5-s10: Here you couldn't even blame the laws of nature, although all the same the laws of nature had constantly, more than anything, wronged me throughout my life.
sentences.p1-5-s37: So these are once again the laws of nature.
sentences.p1-5-s38: So what, then, is the result in the end?
sentences.p1-5-s40: Remember: just the other day I was talking about revenge.
sentences.p1-5-s1: Well, is it possible — really possible — for a person to respect himself even a little if he, even in the very feeling of his own humiliation, attempted to seek pleasure?
sentences.p1-5-s2: I'm not speaking now out of any sickly kind of repentance.
sentences.p1-5-s3: Besides, in general I couldn't bear to say, "Forgive me, Papa, I won't do it again," — not because I was incapable of saying it, but, on the contrary, perhaps precisely because I had been all too able to say it — and how!
sentences.p1-5-s4: As if on purpose I would blunder into it, it used to happen, in such a case when I myself was not the least to blame.
sentences.p1-5-s33: Where are the original causes I can lean on, where are the foundations?
sentences.p1-5-s34: Where am I to get them from?
sentences.p1-5-s35: I exercise my mind, and consequently every original cause in me immediately drags another after it, an even more original one, and so on to infinity.
sentences.p1-5-s36: That is exactly the essence of every consciousness and of thinking.
sentences.p1-5-s11: It's disgusting to remember all this, and it was disgusting even then.
sentences.p1-5-s12: After all, within a minute or so I would already, with malice, realize — as used to happen — that it was all a lie, a lie, an abhorrent, affected lie; that is, all those repentances, all those sentimental feelings, all those vows of rebirth.
sentences.p1-5-s13: And ask: why did I so mangle and torment myself?
sentences.p1-5-s14: Answer: because it was dreadfully boring to sit with folded hands; so I took to tricks.
sentences.p1-5-s19: All my life I somehow felt drawn to pull tricks like that, so that in the end I even ceased to be master of myself.
sentences.p1-5-s20: Another time I wanted to fall in love by force — even twice.
sentences.p1-5-s21: I did suffer, gentlemen, I assure you.
sentences.p1-5-s22: Deep down in my soul it doesn't seem believable that I'm suffering; a mockery stirs, and yet I do suffer — and what's more, truly, in a thoroughly genuine way; I'm jealous, I'm beside myself.
sentences.p1-5-s16: Observe yourselves more closely, gentlemen, and then you'll understand that it's so.
sentences.p1-5-s17: I used to invent adventures for myself and make up a life,
sentences.p1-5-s18: How many times it happened to me — well, even, say, to get offended, so, for no reason, deliberately; and, you know yourself, it would happen that I got offended for no reason, put on a show against myself, but you'd drive yourself so far that in the end, truly, you'd actually be offended.
sentences.p1-5-s25: And it's all from boredom, gentlemen, all from boredom; inertia crushed us.
sentences.p1-5-s26: After all, the direct, legitimate, immediate fruit of consciousness is inertia — that is, the deliberate, folded-arms sitting.
sentences.p1-5-s27: I have already mentioned this above.
sentences.p1-5-s28: I repeat, I repeat emphatically: all immediate people and men of action are active precisely because they are dull and limited.
sentences.p1-5-s42: They say: a person takes revenge because he finds justice in it.
sentences.p1-5-s43: So, he has found the original cause, he has found the basis, namely: justice.
sentences.p1-5-s44: Therefore, he is calm on all sides, and consequently he takes his revenge calmly and successfully, being convinced that he is doing an honest and just deed.
sentences.p1-5-s30: Here's how: because of their narrowness they take the immediate and secondary causes for the primary ones; in this way they are sooner and more easily than others convinced that they have found an unassailable foundation for their cause, and so they calm down — and after all that is the main thing.
sentences.p1-5-s31: After all, to begin acting one must be completely calmed beforehand, and so that there should be no doubts left at all.
sentences.p1-5-s32: Well, how, for example, am I to calm myself?
sentences.p1-5-s57: But what is to be done if the direct and only purpose of every intelligent person is chatter — that is, the deliberate shifting from the empty into the vain.
sentences.p1-5-s45: But after all I see no justice here, nor any virtue either; consequently, if I were to take revenge, it would be only out of anger.
sentences.p1-5-s46: Anger, of course, could have overpowered everything — all my doubts — and so.
sentences.p1-5-s47: It could have served quite successfully in place of the original cause precisely because it is not a cause.
sentences.p1-5-s48: But what is to be done if I don't even have any anger (after all, I only began from this the other day).
sentences.p1-5-s53: The day after tomorrow — that's the latest possible time — you'll begin to despise yourself for having arrogantly duped yourself.
sentences.p1-5-s54: The result: a soap bubble and inertia.
sentences.p1-5-s55: Oh, gentlemen, after all, maybe I only consider myself a clever person because all my life I could neither begin nor finish anything.
sentences.p1-5-s56: All right, let me be a chatterer — a harmless, annoying chatterer, like all of us.
sentences.p1-5-s49: My malice, once again because of those damned laws of consciousness, is subjected to a chemical decomposition.
sentences.p1-5-s50: You watch — the object evaporates, the reasons dissipate, the culprit cannot be found, the grievance ceases to be a grievance and turns into fate, something like a toothache in which no one is guilty; and consequently, once again the same old way out remains — that is, to pound the wall harder.
sentences.p1-5-s51: Well, you'll wave your hand, because you didn't find an initial reason.
sentences.p1-5-s52: But try to get carried away by your feeling blindly, without reasoning, without any initial cause, driving away your consciousness even for that time; come to hate or come to love — just so as not to sit there with your arms folded.
